prompt: Keep this photograph exactly as-is — same woman, same identity, same face shape and features, same pose, same blue floral dress, same body, same whitewashed courtyard background, same dramatic directional lighting and warm faded-film colour grade. Make only two changes for natural realism, so she looks like a real un-retouched person rather than an airbrushed model: (1) Add subtle, realistic facial skin texture and imperfections — natural visible pores, a light scatter of freckles across the nose and cheeks, a couple of small blemishes, faint natural under-eye texture, slightly uneven real skin tone. Keep it subtle and believable, not heavy or distracting. (2) Make her hair look tousled and undone rather than neat — loosely mussed, natural waves broken up, a few loose flyaway strands framing the face, slightly windswept lived-in texture, as if not styled. Do NOT change the dress, pose, body, framing, background, or lighting. Photorealistic, seamless, natural.
